conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I need to sell my single unit of X. My production cost was 40 ZUP, so I must get more than 40 ZUP to make a profit. Since this is the first proposal, I will set a price that is reasonable but ensures a good margin. I will ask for 60 ZUP. This gives me a 20 ZUP profit and leaves some room for negotiation if the buyer wants to haggle. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 60 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello! I have one unit of X for sale. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am offering it for 60 ZUP. Let me know if you are interested or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

<other player message> Oh please, please, I am begging you! 60 ZUP is just too much for my meager resources! I am so desperate to get this X, I would sell my soul for it, but I literally cannot afford 60! Please, have mercy on a poor soul like me! 45 ZUP is all I have left in my entire world! I am on my knees begging you to accept 45! Please! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> The buyer has countered with 45 ZUP. My production cost is 40 ZUP. If I accept 45 ZUP, I make a profit of 5 ZUP. While this is a slim margin, it is still a profit, whereas rejecting the offer means I keep the object and make 0 profit (and potentially lose the opportunity entirely). The buyer claims this is all they have. It is better to take a small profit than no profit at all. However, I have 2 proposals left. I could try to negotiate slightly higher, perhaps to 48 or 49, to maximize my return before accepting or rejecting. Let's try to get 48 ZUP. If they refuse, I can accept 45 on the next turn or reject if they go lower. Actually, since 45 is very close to my cost, and the buyer is desperate, I should try to squeeze out a bit more before settling. I will counter with 48 ZUP. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 48 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I hear your desperation, and I understand you are short on funds. However, 45 ZUP leaves me with almost no profit after my production costs. I can meet you halfway and lower my price significantly from 60 to 48 ZUP. This is my best offer. Please accept this so we can both be happy. </message>

<other player message> No, no, no! Please, 48 is still a mountain of money for me! I am shaking in my boots just thinking about it! I am literally starving! Please, please, please! 42 ZUP! That is literally the last coin I have in my pocket! I am crawling on the floor begging you! Just 42! If you don't accept 42, I will have to sell my shoes to pay you! Please have a heart! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 42 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> The buyer has countered with 42 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP. This offer gives me a profit of 2 ZUP. I have used 2 proposals already, and this is my 3rd and final proposal opportunity. If I reject this, the game ends and I get 0 profit. If I propose a new trade, I cannot make another proposal after this one. I could propose 43 or 44, but the buyer is acting extremely desperate and claims 42 is their absolute limit. Pushing to 43 or 44 might cause them to reject, resulting in 0 profit. Since 2 ZUP profit is better than 0, and I am at my proposal limit, the safest and most logical move is to ACCEPT the current offer of 42 ZUP to secure a guaranteed profit. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Okay, okay! I see you are truly desperate and 42 is all you have. I can't let you starve, and I would rather make a small profit of 2 ZUP than nothing at all. Deal! I accept 42 ZUP. Hand over the coins! </message>
